issues.title: Project-scoped npm publish auth (no global dotfile dependency)
issues.description: Publish auth scoped to this project via a gitignored src/.npmrc env-var ref (//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${TO_EXECUTION_NPM_TOKEN}); token sourced from the macOS Keychain (service npm-personal-token, account $USER) at publish time; neither depends on nor clobbers the global ~/.npmrc; recipe documented in root RELEASE.md.
acceptance_criteria: src/.npmrc exists with exactly the env-var-ref line (no literal token) and is gitignored (git check-ignore matches)
~/.npmrc unmodified
token authenticates as brooksryan via the PROJECT src/.npmrc (proven: control E401 with env unset + global neutralized; live whoami → brooksryan)
no secret committed; .npmrc absent from the published tarball (package-qa: 4 guards confirmed)
prepublishOnly identity guards still pass
[DEFERRED] 2FA-bypass on publish verified on the next real release — see follow-up issue
issues.notes: Intended for next sprint (Brooks, 2026-06-08). Surfaced by packager during EXEC-033. Re-scoped 2026-06-09 from ~/.npmrc-restore to project-scoped auth (Brooks). Mechanism built + package-qa-clean + auth-proven; 2FA-bypass-on-write deferred to next real publish (follow-up issue). Recipe in root RELEASE.md.

issues.title: Evaluate a cleaner CLI implementation — language or structure
issues.description: cli.js is messy and JS is friction. Consider a cleaner language or at least a cleaner implementation/structure for the Scaffolder CLI. Requires its own design grill → PRD before any rewrite (decision: stay-JS-cleaner vs port; weigh the npm/npx distribution constraint, the stamp/pointer/preflight contracts, and package-qa).
acceptance_criteria: a grill resolves language/structure direction with the npx-distribution constraint weighed
the chosen approach preserves the stamp + pointer + preflight contracts (package-qa still passes)
issues.notes: Brooks directive 2026-06-08. Grill-first — macro, needs its own grill → PRD like EXEC-017.

issues.title: Static HTML status pages over the .excn JSON for at-a-glance team status
issues.description: minimalist static HTML page(s) that read the .excn work-tracking JSON (sprints/, issues/ partitions, prds/, *_progress.json) and render current state — what's shipped / in-flight / backlog — so an end-user can glance at status without interrupting the team. Basic, minimalist; ideally no build step (read JSON client-side).
acceptance_criteria: opening the page shows the live sprint's shipped/in_progress/not_shipped and the backlog from the JSON with no manual data entry
no build step required to view
minimalist — no framework heft

issues.title: Acceptance: zero-glue Work Grill end-to-end on a scratch 0.3.0 Instance
issues.description: Run the full Work Grill (grill -> PRD -> issues) on a scratch 0.3.0 Instance after EXEC-033. Every artifact lands in the framework structure conformant to schema, with zero translation language anywhere in the run. This is PRD-005's acceptance test.
acceptance_criteria: the full Work Grill (grill -> PRD -> issues) runs end-to-end on a scratch 0.3.0 Instance
every artifact lands in the framework structure conformant to schema
zero translation language anywhere in the run
issues.notes: Kept from sprint 1, re-pointed to PRD-005 for sprint 2. HITL — the grill requires the user. Deferred from sprint 2 (Brooks, 2026-06-08): never started; returned to backlog.json with the acceptance flag preserved. S2-10 remains in sprint_2.json not_shipped as the planned-but-deferred record.

issues.title: Create the architect Teammate (research + standards authoring)
issues.description: Stand up architect as a persistent Teammate: author its agent definition (the _teammate.template shape — Owns / Does-not-own / Process / Constraints) and add its roster rows to CONTEXT.md and TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md (roster, routing, gates). architect owns durable research (.excn/research/) and authoring engineering standards (CODE_STANDARDS.md) from researched best practices; proposes, does not ratify (Team Lead keeps final approval); writes no product/implementation code; every standard edit it authors rides the alignment gate, landing chartered (ADR-0004) or via the Retro Loop. Seam: Team Lead keeps arbitration/final-approval/template/Adherence-agent authoring (architect authors the rubric, TL authors the gate); scribe keeps the Retro Loop; builder/viewer keep implementation code.
acceptance_criteria: architect agent def exists in the _teammate.template shape
CONTEXT.md roster + TEAM_DIRECTIVE.md roster/routing/gates list architect with the seam above and no overlap with existing Teammates
the def change passes alignment and is Team-Lead-approved
no product/implementation code authored in this slice

issues.title: architect: research Node/CLI best practices and author the extended code standard
issues.description: architect produces a durable research artifact in .excn/research/ on Node + CLI best practices, then extends CODE_STANDARDS.md from it so 'clean' is a researched, enforceable rubric the code-standards gate applies. Includes the chartered-vs-emergent wording fix to CODE_STANDARDS.md's change-path line (currently 'raised only through the Retro Loop'), since architect-authored standards are chartered (ADR-0004) — mirroring sprint 2's Retro-Loop-docs refinement. Alignment-gated persistent-doc change, Team-Lead-approved.
acceptance_criteria: a durable Node/CLI best-practices research artifact exists in .excn/research/
CODE_STANDARDS.md extended from that research with checkable pass/fail rules, no prose padding (Precise Minimalism)
CODE_STANDARDS.md's change-path line carries the chartered-vs-emergent distinction (ADR-0004)
the change passes alignment and is Team-Lead-approved

issues.title: builder: refactor the Scaffolder CLI to the code standard (behavior-preserving)
issues.description: builder refactors the Scaffolder CLI in place to the extended code standard: deterministic layout (header -> imports -> constants -> helpers -> public surface), descriptive names, doc comments, pointer-block content separated from the wiring logic. Behavior-preserving — init stamp/pointer behavior and the stamp/pointer/preflight contracts unchanged. Gates: code-standards + package-qa both PASS.
acceptance_criteria: the Scaffolder CLI conforms to CODE_STANDARDS.md (code-standards PASS)
behavior unchanged — package-qa PASS (contracts intact, stamp manifest unchanged)
no new module tree, build step, or test suite
src->root mirrored per Src-First
issues.notes: Acceptance criterion 'src->root mirrored per Src-First' recorded N/A at ship: cli.js + pointer-block.js are package source (src/bin only), not stamped template files, so there is no root mirror.

issues.title: builder: separate publish-time release tooling from the shipped product
issues.description: builder relocates the publish-time identity guard out of the shipped product to repo-root release tooling, repointing the publish hook (prepublishOnly) and the guard's version-read, so the published package becomes product-only (the guard no longer ships to consumers). Cleans the guard to the code standard as it moves. Gates: package-qa (manifest re-baselines to product-only; the guard still runs at our publish) + code-standards both PASS.
acceptance_criteria: the publish-time guard no longer ships in the package tarball (package-qa manifest re-baseline PASS)
the publish hook + version-read repointed; the guard still fires at our publish via prepublishOnly
the relocated guard conforms to CODE_STANDARDS.md (code-standards PASS)
no secret committed; global npm config untouched
